hvtiPropensityScores 0.0.0.9000
  • Get started

Package index

Package Overview

Package-level documentation and the base S3 class shared by all hvtiPropensityScores data objects.

hvtiPropensityScores-package hvtiPropensityScores
hvtiPropensityScores: Propensity Score Methods for Cardiac Surgery Studies
is_ps_data()
Test whether an object is a ps_data instance

Data

Synthetic datasets for examples and unit tests.

sample_ps_data()
Generate sample data for propensity score examples
sample_ps_data_ordinal()
Generate sample data with an ordinal treatment for ps_ordinal() examples
sample_ps_data_nominal()
Generate sample data with a nominal treatment for ps_nominal() examples
sample_ps_data_count()
Generate sample data with a count outcome for bs_count() examples

Propensity Score Estimation

Functions for estimating propensity scores and balancing scores from raw patient data. Supports binary, ordinal, nominal, continuous, and count outcomes, with optional multiple-imputation averaging.

ps_logistic()
Estimate propensity scores via binary logistic regression
ps_ordinal()
Estimate propensity scores via ordinal (cumulative logit) logistic regression
ps_nominal()
Estimate propensity scores via nominal (generalised logit) logistic regression
bs_continuous()
Estimate a balancing score via linear regression
bs_count()
Estimate a balancing score via negative-binomial or Poisson regression

Propensity Score Methods

Functions for balancing treated and control groups via matching or inverse-probability-of-treatment weighting.

ps_match()
Propensity Score Nearest-Neighbour Matching
ps_weight()
Inverse-Probability-of-Treatment Weighting (IPTW)

Sensitivity Analysis

Functions for assessing robustness of propensity score analyses to unmeasured confounding and model assumptions.

sa_rosenbaum()
Rosenbaum Sensitivity Bounds for Matched Analyses
sa_evalue()
E-Value Sensitivity Analysis
sa_overlap()
Propensity Score Overlap and Positivity Diagnostics
sa_trim_sweep()
IPTW Weight-Trimming Sensitivity Sweep

Internal

S3 methods for printing and summarising ps_data objects.

print(<ps_data>)
Print a ps_data object
summary(<ps_data>)
Summarise a ps_data object

On this page

Developed by John Ehrlinger.
